title: SAP Fico Implementation Manager
description:
Responsibilities:

  • Acts as a strategic liaison between Group IT and the enterprise’s back office functions, including Finance, Treasury, Tax, HR, Legal, Corporate Affairs, and Audit
  • Identify Back office business needs, translating them into IT-enabled solutions, and ensuring the successful delivery and adoption of digital initiatives that enhance operational efficiency and compliance.
  • Facilitate regular Back office cadence meetings (e.g. monthly/bi-monthly) with country teams to track SAP, Back office, HCM related digital transformation progress
  • Drive automation of Back office (including HR) using tools like RPA, OCR, and SAP HCM etc
  • Lead the design and implementation of SAP HCM solutions, including Personnel Administration (PA), Organizational Management (OM), Time Management (TM), and Payroll, with a focus on automation and process standardization.
  • Drive end-to-end Back office (including HR) digitalization initiatives by identifying manual Back office (including HR) processes and implementing automation opportunities using SAP and complementary tools.
  • Collaborate with Back office (including HR) business partners and process owners to gather requirements, translate them into technical specifications, and deliver system solutions that meet business needs.
  • Serve as the primary point of contact for SAP HCM configuration, troubleshooting, and enhancements in the Singapore office.
  • Support change management, user training, and post-implementation support for new HR automation tools and workflows.
  • Coordinate with regional and global IT teams to ensure alignment with enterprise architecture and data governance policies.
  • Ensure HRIS data quality, integrity, and compliance with regulatory and internal audit requirements.
  • Provide insights and recommendations for continuous improvement of HR systems and processes.
  • Lead cross-functional collaboration between HR, IT, Finance, and external partners to drive system alignment with enterprise architecture, data security, and audit requirements.
  • Spearhead change management efforts, including stakeholder engagement, user adoption strategies, and post-deployment support for HR automation tools and initiatives.
  • Establish performance metrics and benchmarks to assess system effectiveness and drive continuous improvement in HR service delivery

Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Informa on Systems, Human Resources, Business, or a related field; advanced certifications (e.g. SAP HCM, PMP) are advantageous.
  • Minimum 7–10 years of experience in SAP (FI/CO, HCM), RPA implementation and op miza on, with demonstrated leadership in HR technology transformation.
  • 5+ years of experience in IT business partnering or project management within a multinational environment.
  • Proven track record in driving Back oﬃce, HR automation and digitalization projects, including third-party tool integrations (e.g. RPA, SuccessFactors, time/attendance systems).
  • Strong business acumen with deep understanding of Back oﬃce, HR functional processes, compliance frameworks, and regional practices
  • Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills, with the ability to engage at both strategic and operational levels.
  • Experience in Agile or hybrid project delivery models is preferred.
  • Excellent communica on and stakeholder management skills.
  • Experience working with cross-functional teams
